anzsic_codes.name: Rigid and Semi-Rigid Polymer Product Manufacturing
Wooden Furniture and Upholstered Seat Manufacturing

merger_description:
The Carlyle Group Inc. (**Carlyle**) is Delaware incorporated publicly traded company listed on the NASDAQ, incorporated under Delaware law, with registered address in 1001 Pennsylvania Avenue, NW Washington, DC, 20004-2505, United States.

Carlyle manages funds that invest globally across three investment disciplines: (i) Global Private Equity: (i) Global Private Equity; (ii) Global Credit (including liquid credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and (iii) via its 100% indirect affiliate AlpInvest – Investment Solutions (private equity fund of funds program, which includes primary fund, secondary fund, portfolio finance and related co-investment activities).

Carlyle operates across the globe but is predominantly active in the EU and the US. Funds managed or advised by Carlyle have controlling interests in a number of portfolio companies that have activities in Australia.

CHN Group is a South Korean group of companies active globally in the manufacturing and supply of home/domestic appliances and related services, with a focus on water purifiers and water purification filters. To a limited extent, CHN Group is active in the manufacturing and sale of other home appliances (air purifiers, air filters, coffee machines, coffee capsules, bidet, mattresses, bed frames, bed foundations and massage chairs).

CHN Group consists of Chung Ho Nais Co., Ltd., MCM Co., Ltd., Microfilter Co., Ltd. and their respective subsidiaries. In Australia, CHN Group supplies its products to vendors on a business-to-business basis only. CHN Group has subsidiaries in the People's Republic of China, Vietnam, Malaysia, and Mexico.

Carlyle, through a special acquisition vehicle, Crystal Holdings Korea Co., Ltd., will acquire sole control over the CHN Group from Kyung Eun Lee, Sang Hoon Joung, Whi Chul Joung, and Seonghoon Jeong.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Crystal Holdings Korea Co., Ltd, a special acquisition vehicle
indirectly owned by affiliates of The Carlyle Group Inc. (Carlyle),
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of Chung Ho Nais Co., Ltd., MCM Co., Ltd., Microfilter Co.,
Ltd., and their respective subsidiaries (together, CHN Group), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Carlyle is Delaware incorporated publicly traded company listed on
the NASDAQ, registered in the United States.
Carlyle manages funds that invest globally across three investment
disciplines: (i) Global Private Equity; (ii) Global Credit (including liquid
credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and (iii) via its 100%
indirect affiliate AlpInvest – Investment Solutions (private equity
fund of funds program, which includes primary fund, secondary fund,
portfolio finance and related co-investment activities).
Carlyle operates across the globe but is predominantly active in the
EU and the US. Funds managed or advised by Carlyle have
controlling interests in a number of portfolio companies that have
activities in Australia.
CHN Group is a South Korean group of companies active globally in
the manufacturing and supply of home/domestic appliances and
related services, with a focus on water purifiers and water
purification filters. To a limited extent, CHN Group is active in the
manufacturing and sale of other home appliances (air purifiers, air
filters, coffee machines, coffee capsules, bidets, mattresses, bed
frames, bed foundations and massage chairs). In Australia, the CHN
Group supplies its products to vendors on a business-to-business
basis only.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal or
vertical overlap between Carlyle (including funds managed or
advised by Carlyle) and CHN Group in the supply of manufacturing
and supply of domestic appliances and related services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
